data.title: As a DSO maintainer, I add real-URL end-to-end installer validation (resolves bug 068c-1e8a)
data.description:
## Description

What: Add a real-URL end-to-end test path for the installer so that template repo drift or installer breakage fails loudly in CI instead of silently passing via mocked git. Resolves bug 068c-1e8a.
Why: Current smoke test stubs git, making the real clone vacuous. Provides the ongoing validation loop that the original epic shipped without.
Scope:
- IN: Extend tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh with an opt-in real-URL test mode (or add a separate test file) that exercises bash with real curl against the live template repo without stubbing git; CI configuration for scheduled or release-gated execution.
- OUT: Fixing unrelated stubbing in other smoke tests; full CI test-tiering redesign.

## Done Definitions

- When complete, a real-URL e2e test exists that does not stub git and exercises real git clone against the real template URL.
  Satisfies: Real-URL end-to-end validation (SC7)
- When complete, the RED phase (test run after S1 while the repo exists but has no rendered content) confirms failure, and the GREEN phase (test run after S2, S3, S4 complete) confirms pass. (Applied from adversarial review F4 - multi-phase dependency.)
  Satisfies: SC7
- When complete, bug 068c-1e8a is closed with a reference to the new test path.
  Satisfies: SC7
- When complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Testing] Real-URL tests must be opt-in or scheduled (nightly or release-gated) to avoid flaky network-dependent CI.
- [Reliability] Network failures must not fail regular CI runs - separate test tier.
- [Sequencing] F4: mark as multi-phase. Author RED after S1, transition to GREEN only after S2+S3+S4 merged. Do not wait until S4 to begin; begin writing test harness after S1.

## Escalation Policy

Escalation policy: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue - one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
